chunks.text: 32-bit or 64-bit The basic idea is that to use a 64-bit version of Eclipse, you must have a 64-bit JVM and an operating system that will support 64-bit applications. When in doubt, choose the 32-bit version of Eclipse, it is more l

the 32-bit version of Eclipse, it is more likely to work than the 64-bit version. 32-bit or 64-bit? Windows 8 : Oddly, Microsoft does not appear to have instructions for how to determine if Windows 8 is 32-bit. Instead, see http://pcs

if Windows 8 is 32-bit. Instead, see http://pcsupport.about.com/od/windows-8/a/windows-8-64-bit-32-bit.htm for details Windows 7 or Vista : "Start", then right click on "Computer", then click on "Properties". Under System, you should see the s

"Properties". Under System, you should see the system type. The 64-bit version of Windows 7 will say "64-bit Operating System". See http://windows.microsoft.com/en-US/windows7/32-bit-and-64-bit-Windows-frequently-asked-questions . for details.

indows-frequently-asked-questions . for details. Windows XP : "Start", then right click on "My Computer", and then click on "Properties". Under "System", if you see "x64 Edition", then you are running a 64-bit version of Windows. If "x64 Edition
ing a 64-bit version of Windows. If "x64 Edition" is not listed, then you are running a 32-bit version of Windows. Return to the Installing Eclipse page.
